---
title: Tea Tasting Statisticians
listing:
  contents: "posts/*/index.md"
  type: default
  sort: "date desc"
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: true
  categories: true
  feed: false
---

## Mission

Tea Tasting Statisticians is a practical biostatistics and regulatory science blog for **MedTech, pharma, and clinical teams**.
We translate FDA/MFDS/ICH guidance into operational decisions for study design, evidence strategy, and submission readiness.
If you are a clinician, medical affairs lead, clinical operations manager, or product owner without a statistics degree, this site is built for you.

## Start Here

- [Start Here](/start-here/): onboarding path by role and common questions.
- [FDA Guidance Digest](/guidance-watch/): curated, high-signal guidance summaries in Korean and English.
- [Problem Index](/tags/): pick a practical problem and jump to relevant posts.

## Featured: FDA Guidance Digest

- [Considerations for the use of the Plausible Mechanism Framework to Develop Individualized Therapies that Target Specific Genetic Conditions with Known Biological Cause](/posts/Considerations_for_the_use_of_the_Plausible_Mechanism_Framework_to_Develop_Indiv/)  
  Why this matters: clarifies what evidence logic regulators expect when classic RCT pathways are constrained.
- [FDA Guidance Statistical Guidance on Reporting Results from Studies Evaluating Diagnostic Tests](/posts/FDA-Guidance-Statistical-Guidance-on-Reporting-Results-from-Studies-Evaluating-Diagnostic-Tests_Translated/)  
  Why this matters: helps non-statisticians understand what must be explicit in diagnostic test reporting.
- [Use of Real-World Evidence to Support Regulatory Decision-Making for Medical Devices](/posts/RWE_medical_device_FDA/)  
  Why this matters: shows how RWE can support device submissions and where review risk usually appears.

## Who Writes This

::: {.callout-note}
**Tea Tasting Lady**  
Senior biostatistician with 10+ years in regulatory science, including 7 years at MFDS and 5 years in industry.

**Tea Tasting Gentleman**  
Biostatistician and PhD candidate in clinical epidemiology focused on diabetes, RWE, and innovative trial designs.
:::

## Browse Posts

Use the filter box and category chips below to search posts directly from the home page.
For the full archive view, go to [All Posts](/posts/).

## Stay Connected

- Follow authors on LinkedIn: [Tea Tasting Lady](https://www.linkedin.com/in/systat/) and [Tea Tasting Gentleman](https://www.linkedin.com/in/shstat1729/)
- Subscribe by RSS: [posts feed](/posts/index.xml)
- Discuss posts in comments (Giscus) and share to your team with the LinkedIn share button on each post.
